draft.introduction: 1 Thessalonians 5:16–28 concludes Paul’s earliest letter with a rapid series of imperatives—“rejoice always, pray without ceasing, give thanks in all circumstances.” These became foundational for the hesychast prayer tradition. From Codex Sinaiticus.

translation.text: Rejoice always, pray without ceasing, give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you. Do not quench the Spirit. Do not despise prophecies, but test everything; hold fast what is good. Abstain from every form of evil.
translation.original_text: παντοτε χαιρετε αδιαλειπτως προσευχεσθε εν παντι ευχαριστειτε

translation.text: Now may the God of peace himself sanctify you completely, and may your whole spirit and soul and body be kept blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ. He who calls you is faithful; he will surely do it.
translation.original_text: αυτος δε ο θεος της ειρηνης αγιασαι υμας ολοτελεις

translator_notes.note: The command to “pray without ceasing” (αδιαλείπτως προσεύχεσθε) became the cornerstone text for the Eastern Orthodox hesychast tradition and the Jesus Prayer. The anonymous Russian classic The Way of a Pilgrim takes this verse as its starting point.
